behavior_summary: Provides the examine ('look') and use ('drink') behavior for bottle items. The look handler reports a short name based on the bottle's frame; the use handler applies a feed/healing effect to the avatar, marks the bottle as used, plays short barks (e.g. 'Burp...'), and coordinates a longer timed drunken sequence.

Drinking logic inspects the item's frame to choose effect strength and text, reads/writes an item quantity/state (Q) to ensure one-time use behaviors, and calls FeedAvatar() to apply immediate benefit.

A spawned helper coroutine implements time-sequenced drunken behavior: it sets/advances a global gettingDrunk flag, plays intermittent barks/messages and animations/effects (via multiple spawned METHODS and FREE helpers), manipulates avatar stasis and references (resetRef, setAvatarInStasis), and clears state when finished.

Randomization (urandom) and actor state queries (Npc::isBusy, Npc::getLastAnimSet, Npc::getDir) are used to vary which animations/effects and messages occur during the drunken sequence and to avoid interrupting conversations (somebodyTalking).
notable_control_flow: BOTTLE::look branches on Item::getFrame() (frames 5..9 have specific names; otherwise fallback to generic 'bottle').
BOTTLE::use checks Item::getQ(); if Q==0 it sets Q to 1 and proceeds with drinking side effects; otherwise it still barks and may still grant effects depending on frame (inferred).

Use updates or reads the global gettingDrunk flag to decide whether to spawn a new U8P_BOTTLE::BOTTLE::0562 coroutine or to advance an existing drunken-state value (values 0..3 are used to represent stages).
The drunken coroutine loops with a small local counter and randomized offsets; it conditionally spawns animation/effect routines and schedules delayed frees to sequence timed messages and cleanup.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning and possible ranges of the item 'Q' field (Item::getQ / Item::setQ) beyond the observed used==0 check — is it a usage counter, boolean or quantity?
Exact gameplay effects of each spawned METHOD (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::091C, METHOD::087D) — these appear to be animations/effects but their concrete visual or mechanical effects are not decoded here.

Detailed mapping of bottle frame values to in-game potion types, exact feed amounts per frame, and any side effects beyond FeedAvatar() (the general mapping is inferred from branches but exact numerics are not all listed).
Interaction semantics with conversation guard somebodyTalking: how aggressively the drunken coroutine defers or suppresses messages when someone is talking is only inferred from reads of that flag.
Whether repeated use of the same bottle after Q is set produces reduced or no effect in all cases (some branches suggest one-time marking, but full behavior across frames is not fully enumerated).

main_function.name: U8P_BOTTLE::look (Event 0)
main_function.purpose: Handle examine/look events for a bottle item: read the bottle frame and emit a short identifying bark (name) appropriate to that frame.
entry_conditions: Triggered when a player/actor inspects/looks at the bottle (look event).
Runs in the item's process context and uses the passed-in viewer/process id (pid) and item/process pointer.

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::look (Event 0)
function_map.role: Examine/description handler that chooses a short name string by frame and calls Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::use (Event 1)
function_map.role: Handle using/drinking the bottle: update item Q, bark, call FeedAvatar, and coordinate spawning of the timed drunken coroutine and animation/effect spawns.

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::0562
function_map.role: Spawned coroutine that implements the multi-step timed drunken behavior (barks, animations, stasis and cleanup).

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the item's frame/type used to select names/effects.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short text message from the item/process to a target pid.

function_map.name: FeedAvatar
function_map.role: Apply immediate benefit/effect to avatar (feeding/healing) invoked on drink use.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawned to run the long-running drunken sequence after drinking (coroutine).

external_calls.purpose: Read bottle type/frame so the handler can pick the correct short name to display.
Display the chosen bottle name/description to the viewer pid.
Check whether the bottle is already used/empty (guard Q==0 vs Q>0).
Mark the bottle's Q field (e.g., mark as used) when consumed.
Apply immediate feed/healing effect to the avatar; amount varies by bottle frame.
Schedule the drunken coroutine and various animation/effect helper routines and delayed frees.
Reset world/reference entries during cleanup of the drunken sequence.
Temporarily freeze/unfreeze the avatar during portions of the drunken animation/sequence.
Randomize timings and choices (hiccups, which animations to spawn).
Query actor facing direction to spawn direction-aware animations/effects.
Spawn an animation/effect for the drunk actor (exact effect inferred).
Schedule a delayed free/cleanup or timed message as part of the drinking sequence.
Schedule another delayed helper/free used during the drunken sequence.

reads.name: somebodyTalking (global [000F 01])
reads.likely_effect: Used as a guard so the drunken coroutine avoids interrupting ongoing dialog or conversation; checks before playing barks/lines.

reads.name: gettingDrunk (global [0024 03])
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether a drunken sequence is already active and to decide whether to spawn or advance state of the drunk coroutine.

writes.name: gettingDrunk (global [0024 03])
writes.likely_effect: Set/advance drunken-state values (0..3) to start the sequence, progress stages, or clear it at the end of the coroutine.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the examine ('look') and use ('drink') behavior for bottle items. The look handler reports a short name based on the bottle's frame; the use handler applies a feed/healing effect to the avatar, marks the bottle as used, plays short barks (e.g. 'Burp...'), and coordinates a longer timed drunken sequence.
